module.label: ACT
module.authorEyebrow: Inspired by The Happiness Trap · Russ Harris

notifications.title: Quiet ACT check-in
notifications.body: Notice an unhelpful thought and unhook from it. A small practice goes a long way.

home.title: ACT
home.subtitle: Inspired by The Happiness Trap by Russ Harris
home.description: Six skills that build psychological flexibility - the ability to act on your values even when difficult thoughts and feelings are present.
home.principlesDescription: Mindfulness skills, values, and committed action - working together.
home.noDefusionLogs: No defusion logs yet. Tap "Defuse a thought" whenever a sticky thought shows up.

defusion.name: Defusion
defusion.desc: Step back from unhelpful thoughts. See them as mental events, not facts.

expansion.name: Acceptance
expansion.desc: Stop fighting difficult emotions. Turn off the Struggle Switch and let them be.

connection.name: Connection
connection.desc: Notice what is happening right now - not the past, not the future.

observingSelf.name: Observing Self
observingSelf.desc: The part of you that notices thoughts and feelings without being defined by them.

values.name: Values
values.desc: Your chosen directions in life. Not goals to achieve - ways to be.

committedAction.name: Committed Action
committedAction.desc: Take values-guided steps even when discomfort is present.

info.attribution: This module is inspired by The Happiness Trap by Russ Harris.

welcome.title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy
welcome.subtitle: ACT means accept what shows up, connect with values, and take effective action
welcome.myth1: Happiness is the natural state for humans
welcome.myth2: If you're not happy, something is wrong with you
welcome.myth3: To have a good life you must eliminate negative feelings
welcome.myth4: You should be able to control your thoughts and feelings
welcome.body: These four myths set a trap: the harder we try to avoid pain, the more we suffer. The mind evolved for survival, not happiness. Negative thoughts and feelings are a built-in feature, not a malfunction.
welcome.controlParadoxBody: Fighting unwanted thoughts and feelings intensifies them. Trying to fix feelings from the inside creates a vicious cycle.

choicePoint.title: The Choice Point
choicePoint.body: In any situation, difficult thoughts and feelings can show up and hook us. When we're hooked, we drift into away moves. Unhooking gives us the choice to make a toward move instead.
choicePoint.away: Away moves: what you do when hooked that takes you away from the life you want.
choicePoint.toward: Toward moves: what you'd do as the person you want to be.

model.title: The ACT Model
model.subtitle: Three moves. One formula.
model.accept: A - Accept thoughts and feelings, and be present
model.formulaBody: Psychological flexibility is the ability to act on what matters even when difficult thoughts and feelings are present. That is the goal.
model.mindfulnessDesc: Defusion and Acceptance unhook you from thoughts and make room for feelings.
model.valuesDesc: Connecting with what matters most provides direction. Committed action puts it into practice.

concerns.title: What brings you here?
concerns.subtitle: Select everything that applies. This helps us suggest where to start.

bullsEye.title: A quick look at your values
bullsEye.subtitle: How close are you to living your values right now? 1 = far off, 10 = living it fully.

commit.title: Your first step
commit.subtitle: Based on what you shared, we suggest starting here.
commit.switchHint: You can start with any principle - tap to change.
commit.reminderHint: Off by default. You can change this any time in settings.
commit.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

defusion.listSubtitle: A record of thoughts you've stepped back from.
defusion.noLogs: No entries yet. Use defusion when a sticky thought shows up.
defusion.newSubtitle: Step back from the story your mind is telling.
defusion.thoughtPlaceholder: Write the thought as it appeared
defusion.thoughtHint: Don't soften it - write it exactly as your mind said it.
defusion.categoryLabel: What kind of thought is this?
defusion.fusionBeforeLabel: How strongly is this thought pulling you right now?
defusion.fusionBeforeHint: 0 = no pull at all, 100 = completely fused, feels like an absolute fact
defusion.techniqueHint: First time? Try "I'm having the thought that..." - it works for most situations.
defusion.defusedVersionLabel: How does the thought look after the technique? (optional)
defusion.defusedVersionPlaceholder: e.g. "I'm having the thought that I'm going to fail"
defusion.fusionAfterLabel: How strongly is it pulling you now?
defusion.fusionAfterHint: You don't need it to disappear - any reduction counts.
defusion.notesPlaceholder: How did that feel? What did you notice?

techniqueDescriptions.havingTheThoughtThat: Prefix the thought with "I'm having the thought that…" - this one line creates distance instantly.
techniqueDescriptions.musicalThoughts: Silently sing the thought to a silly tune (Happy Birthday, Jingle Bells). Observe what happens.
techniqueDescriptions.namingTheStory: Give the recurring pattern a name: "There's the Not Good Enough story again." Notice it as a story, not a fact.
techniqueDescriptions.thankingYourMind: Say to yourself: "Thanks, mind, for that thought." Acknowledge it without engaging.
techniqueDescriptions.sillyVoices: Hear the thought in a cartoon character's voice. Notice how its urgency shifts.
techniqueDescriptions.televisionScreen: Imagine the words scrolling across a TV screen at the back of your mind. You are watching, not reading.
techniqueDescriptions.subtitles: For distressing mental images: imagine foreign-language subtitles beneath the image. Observe the effect.

defusion.noFusionDrop: Fusion stayed at {{after}}. That's ok - noticing is enough.

expansion.listSubtitle: A record of times you made room for difficult feelings.
expansion.noLogs: No entries yet. Use acceptance when a difficult emotion or sensation is present.
expansion.noUrgeLogs: No urge surfing entries yet. Try it when an urge or craving shows up.
expansion.newSubtitle: Stop fighting the feeling. Make space for it instead.
expansion.urgeSurfSubtitle: Urges rise, peak, and fall. You don't have to act on them.
expansion.emotionHint: Name it as precisely as you can. Where is the line between anxiety and dread for you?
expansion.bodyLabel: Where do you feel it in your body?
expansion.bodyPlaceholder: e.g. tightness in the chest, heaviness in the stomach
expansion.bodyHint: Locate it, then describe its qualities - size, weight, temperature, movement.
expansion.intensityBeforeLabel: How intense is the feeling right now?
expansion.struggleSwitchQuestion: Are you currently fighting this feeling?
expansion.struggleSwitchOn: Yes - I'm resisting or trying to get rid of it
expansion.discomfortTypeLabel: Is this the original feeling, or extra suffering from struggling?
expansion.discomfortTypeHint: The original feeling is what's naturally here. Extra suffering is what gets piled on when we fight it.
expansion.clean: The original feeling, as it is
expansion.dirty: Extra suffering I'm adding by struggling
expansion.techniqueHint: New to this? TAME It with Kindness is the core practice.
expansion.intensityAfterHint: The goal is not to eliminate the feeling - making room is enough.
expansion.notesPlaceholder: What did you notice? Any shift?

techniqueDescriptions.fourStepExpansion: Take note → Allow → Make room → Expand awareness — with kindness. The core acceptance practice from the book.
techniqueDescriptions.acceptanceSelfTalk: Speak to the feeling with compassion: "It's okay to feel this." Let the words soften the resistance.
techniqueDescriptions.acceptanceImagery: Imagine the feeling as an object - its shape, color, texture. Watch it shift when you stop fighting.

fourStepGuide.title: TAME It with Kindness
fourStepGuide.step1Body: Notice and name what's here: "I'm noticing anxiety." Locate where the feeling sits in your body.
fourStepGuide.step2Body: Give the feeling permission to be here, even though you don't like it. Stop trying to push it away.
fourStepGuide.step3Body: Breathe into it and open up around it. Place a hand where you feel it most, as if breathing warmth and kindness into the feeling.
fourStepGuide.step4Body: Keep the feeling in your awareness and, at the same time, broaden your focus to the world around you — what you can see, hear, and do.

expansion.noIntensityDrop: Intensity stayed at {{after}}. Making room is the goal - not eliminating the feeling.

urgeSurf.urgePlaceholder: e.g. to drink, to check my phone, to eat, to avoid
urgeSurf.urgeHint: Describe it plainly. What is pulling you?
urgeSurf.triggerPlaceholder: e.g. stress, boredom, a specific situation
urgeSurf.triggerHint: Optional. What happened just before the urge appeared?
urgeSurf.observeBody:
This is TAME applied to urges: Take note of the urge, Allow it, Make room for it, and Expand your awareness.

Urges are like ocean waves - they rise, peak, and fall. Your job is to surf, not to resist.

Notice where the urge lives in your body. Breathe. Watch it rise. It will peak and then subside.
urgeSurf.peakHint: Rate the urge at its strongest point.
urgeSurf.actedOnLabel: Did you act on the urge?
urgeSurf.notesPlaceholder: How did the wave feel? Did it peak and fall?

connection.listSubtitle: A record of times you came fully into the present moment.
connection.noLogs: No entries yet. Try "Notice Five Things" the next time your mind drifts.
connection.newSubtitle: Bring your full attention to what is happening right now.
connection.techniqueHint: New here? "Notice Five Things" is the quickest way to land in the present.
connection.activityPlaceholder: e.g. walking, eating, talking with a friend
connection.activityHint: Any activity can become a connection practice.
connection.noticesPlaceholder: What you saw, heard, felt, smelled, tasted…
connection.noticesHint: No need to evaluate - just describe what was there.

techniqueDescriptions.noticeFiveThings: 5 things you see · 4 you hear · 3 you feel · 1 you smell or taste. Takes about 60 seconds.
techniqueDescriptions.mindfulActivity: Bring full attention to whatever you're already doing - eating, walking, washing up. No extra time needed.
techniqueDescriptions.tenDeepBreaths: Ten slow, deliberate breaths. Notice the physical sensation of each one. Simple and effective anywhere.
techniqueDescriptions.dropAnchor: Acknowledge · Come back to your body · Engage. A three-step grounding practice for when you feel pulled off course.
techniqueDescriptions.bodyScan: A slow, mindful scan from head to toe. Notice sensations without trying to change anything.

noticeFiveGuide.title: Notice Five Things
noticeFiveGuide.intro: Pause. Look around. Work through the senses one at a time.
noticeFiveGuide.feel: 3 things you can physically FEEL (temperature, texture, pressure)
noticeFiveGuide.smell: 1 thing you can SMELL or TASTE
noticeFiveGuide.outro: You don't have to stop your thoughts - just notice them and return to now.

observingSelf.listSubtitle: A record of moments you accessed the stable witness within.
observingSelf.noLogs: No sessions yet. Try "Ten Deep Breaths" to step into the Observing Self.
observingSelf.newSubtitle: Access the part of you that notices - steady, unjudging, always here.
observingSelf.techniqueHint: "Ten Deep Breaths" is the entry point. Try Sky & Weather once you're familiar.
observingSelf.observedLabel: What did you observe from this vantage point?
observingSelf.observedPlaceholder: Thoughts, feelings, sensations you noticed - without judging them
observingSelf.observedHint: You are not reporting what was bad or good - just what was there.

techniqueDescriptions.tenDeepBreaths: Take ten slow, deep breaths. With each breath, notice what you observe - thoughts, feelings, sensations - from a witness perspective.
techniqueDescriptions.skyAndWeather: The sky & weather metaphor: thoughts and feelings are the weather. You are the sky - vast, unchanged, never harmed by any storm passing through.
techniqueDescriptions.bodyAwareness: Scan the body from feet to head. Notice sensations as a witness - without judgment or the urge to change anything.

tenBreathsGuide.title: Ten Deep Breaths
tenBreathsGuide.instruction: Take a slow, deep breath - right down into your belly.
tenBreathsGuide.inhale: As you breathe IN: say to yourself "I notice I'm having the feeling of ___"
tenBreathsGuide.exhale: As you breathe OUT: look around and notice five things you can see.
tenBreathsGuide.repeat: Repeat for ten breaths. You are the one noticing - not the thoughts or feelings themselves.

skyWeatherGuide.title: Sky & Weather
skyWeatherGuide.body:
Imagine you are the sky. Your thoughts and feelings are the weather.

The weather changes constantly - storms, sunshine, fog, wind. But the sky always has room for it, and no weather, no matter how wild, can harm the sky.

You are not the weather. You are the vast space in which it all comes and goes.

From this perspective, notice what weather is passing through right now.

bodyAwarenessGuide.title: Body Awareness Scan
bodyAwarenessGuide.body:
Start at the soles of your feet. Slowly scan upward: ankles, calves, knees, thighs, hips, belly, chest, shoulders, arms, hands, neck, face.

At each area, simply notice what is there - warmth, tension, tingling, nothing. You are the witness. You are not trying to change anything.

If you notice discomfort, breathe into it and continue scanning.

values.listSubtitle: Your chosen directions in the four key areas of life.
values.domainIntro: Tap a domain to clarify what matters most to you there.
values.domainSubtitle: Define what matters most to you in this area of life.
values.valueStatementHint: Values are directions, not destinations. Describe how you want to be, not what you want to have.
values.currentActionsLabel: What are you already doing that reflects this value?
values.currentActionsHint: Even small actions pointing in the right direction count.
values.desiredActionsLabel: What would you like to do more of?
values.desiredActionsPlaceholder: Actions you want to take or increase
values.desiredActionsHint: Be specific - what would doing more actually look like?
values.barriersHint: Often: difficult thoughts or feelings, lack of time, old habits. Naming them is the first step.
values.importanceRatingLabel: How important is this domain to you? (1–10)
values.importanceRatingHint: 1 = not very important right now, 10 = central to my life
values.alignmentRatingLabel: How aligned is your daily life with this value? (1–10)
values.alignmentRatingHint: 1 = far from it, 10 = living it fully

bullsEye.title: Values check-in
bullsEye.subtitle: Rate how closely your current life matches each value direction. 1 = far off, 10 = living it fully.

program.startDescription: A guided path through four phases — notice your choice points, be present, open up, and do what matters. Go at your own pace.
program.noDailyPractice: No daily task this phase — take your time with the step above.
program.advanceEarlyConfirmBody: You haven't finished this phase's steps yet. Move on anyway?
program.ready: You've finished this phase — ready for the next?
program.abandonDescription: Your logged practices stay saved. You can start the program again any time.
program.graduationBody: You built skills to be present, open up, and do what matters. Keep using them.

foundation.title: Build a foundation
foundation.description: Notice what hooks you, map your toward and away moves, and learn to drop anchor when you're pulled off course.

bePresent.title: Be present
bePresent.description: Come back to the here and now — through your senses, your body, and the part of you that simply notices.

openUp.title: Open up
openUp.description: Unhook from sticky thoughts and make room for difficult feelings instead of fighting them.

doWhatMatters.title: Do what matters
doWhatMatters.description: Clarify what you care about and take small, values-guided steps — even when it's uncomfortable.

tasks.makeRoomOnce: Make room for a feeling once

choicePoint.title: Choice point
choicePoint.empty: Map your first choice point to see what hooks you and where you want to go.
choicePoint.hooksHint: Difficult situations, thoughts, feelings, urges, or memories.
choicePoint.awayHint: What you do that makes things worse in the long run.
choicePoint.towardHint: What you'd do as the person you want to be.
choicePoint.primer: At any moment you can move toward the life you want or away from it. When difficult thoughts and feelings hook you, you get pulled into away moves. Unhooking skills give you the choice.

committedAction.listSubtitle: Values-guided actions you have committed to taking.
committedAction.noActions: No committed actions yet. Tap "New action" to start one.
committedAction.newSubtitle: A small, concrete step in a values direction.
committedAction.titlePlaceholder: e.g. "Go for a 20-minute walk three times a week"
committedAction.titleHint: Be specific and concrete. Vague commitments are hard to act on.
committedAction.descriptionPlaceholder: Anything that helps you remember why and how
committedAction.obstaclesLabel: What might get in the way? (HARD barriers)
committedAction.obstaclesPlaceholder: e.g. Hooked: "I'll fail" → unhook. Avoiding: dread → make room.
committedAction.obstaclesHint: Name the HARD barriers and your answer to each: Hooked by thoughts (unhook), Avoiding discomfort (make room), Remoteness from values (reconnect to your why), Doubtful goals (break it into a smaller, realistic step).
committedAction.stepsSubtitle: Break the action into small, doable steps.
committedAction.noSteps: No steps yet. Add the first small step below.
committedAction.deleteConfirmBody: All steps will also be deleted. This cannot be undone.

dropAnchor.title: Drop anchor
dropAnchor.intro: When you feel pulled off course, drop anchor to steady yourself. Repeat the cycle as many times as you need.
dropAnchor.acknowledge: Acknowledge your thoughts and feelings. Silently name what's here: "I'm noticing anxiety."
dropAnchor.connect: Come back to your body. Press your feet into the floor, straighten your spine, and breathe.
dropAnchor.engage: Engage with the world. Notice where you are, what you can see and hear, and refocus.
